name: Anglo American plc
other_listings: LSE: AAL (primary); JSE: AGL (secondary); also OTC ADR (AAUKF/NGLOY)
reporting_currency: USD (US dollars); LSE-primary in GBp, JSE-listed in ZAR
registered_address: 17 Charterhouse Street, London EC1N 6RA, United Kingdom

results_calendar.q1_production_report: Late April (Q1 2026 reported 28 Apr 2026; guidance maintained)

operating_assets: Copper: Collahuasi & Los Bronces (Chile), Quellaveco (Peru) - core growth engine
Premium Iron Ore: Kumba Iron Ore (South Africa, ~70% held, JSE: KIO) + Minas-Rio (Brazil)
Crop Nutrients: Woodsmith polyhalite project (UK) - slowed pre-FID, Mitsubishi up to 25%
De Beers (~85%) - IN FORMAL SALE PROCESS (diamonds)
Steelmaking coal - being sold to Dhilmar (up to $3.88bn, agreed 18 May 2026)
Nickel - being sold to MMG (up to $500m)

corporate_actions.anglo_teck_merger: All-share merger of equals with Teck Resources (announced 9 Sep 2025). 1.3301 Anglo shares per Teck share; Anglo holders ~62.4% / Teck ~37.6%. Creates 'Anglo Teck' (HQ Vancouver, primary-listed London), >70% copper, ~$800m/yr synergies. $4.5bn special dividend (~$4.19/sh) to Anglo holders pre-completion. Approvals: shareholders, BC Supreme Court, Investment Canada Act, South Korea CLEARED; China (SAMR) antitrust outstanding. Completion guided Sep 2026-Mar 2027.
corporate_actions.valterra_platinum: PGMs demerged as Valterra Platinum (May 2025); residual stake sold Sep 2025.
corporate_actions.de_beers: ~85% stake in formal sale; lead bidder a consortium led by ex-De Beers MD Gareth Penny backed by Qatari funds (Mayhoola, Al Mirqab); targeted ~H2 2026.

notes: USD reporter, Dec year-end. PRIMARY listing is LSE (AAL); JSE secondary (AGL). FY2025 (per RNS 20 Feb 2026): revenue $18,546m (+4.5%), underlying EBITDA $6,417m (+1.5%), attributable LOSS $(3,700)m (driven by $2.3bn De Beers impairment; cumulative De Beers writedowns ~$6.8bn over two years), underlying EPS continuing 80c, capex $3,322m, attributable FCF $790m, net debt $8,571m (-19.3%), net debt/EBITDA 1.3x, DPS 23c (-64.1%), ROCE 12.0%. Segment EBITDA: Copper $3,983m (695kt, -10%), Premium Iron Ore $2,873m (60.8Mt), Manganese $127m, Crop Nutrients $(66)m, De Beers $(511)m. FY2026 copper guidance trimmed to 700,000-760,000t (from 760,000-820,000t). Statutory identifiers (ISIN/registration/LEI/CIK) left null pending verification on a deep run.

FY2025 headline figures verified instead against the published FY2025 results (RNS, 20 Feb 2026) and the fund's existing Anglo model. Retry the original-binary save from an environment with direct outbound HTTP (deep run).

Anglo_American_AAL_12-18m_Outlook_Hormuz._provenance: Internal IC briefing (quick mode): 12-18m outlook, management risks, management incentives, and Strait-of-Hormuz Scenario A (stays closed) vs B (opens in weeks). Built from Anglo FY2025 results (RNS 20 Feb 2026), Q1 2026 update, the fund's existing Anglo model, and current macro (copper/iron ore/Brent/Hormuz/remuneration) research.

Anglo_American_AAL_2yr_Outlook_Bull_Bear._provenance: Earlier IC briefing (14 Jun 2026): bull/bear 3-year growth projections, Anglo Teck merger, Iran-war impact. Companion model: Anglo_American_Growth_Projections_Jun2026.xlsx. Superseded for the Hormuz-scenario view by the 17 Jun 2026 12-18m memo.

Anglo_American_Growth_Projections_Jun202._provenance: Formula-driven bull/base/bear growth projection model accompanying the 14 Jun 2026 memo (commodity deck -> EBITDA/FCF/net debt; Teck overlay tab).
